content.rendered:
1973: Bruce and the E Street Band play their third night of a week-long residency at Oliver’s in Boston.

1975: Bruce and the boys play their second of three nights at Alex Cooley’s Electric Ballroom in Atlanta.

1976: The original Miami Horns (also known as the Asbury Jukes horn section) make their final appearance with Bruce and the E Street Band at their show at the Springfield Civic Center.

1978: At Night Two of a three-night Darkness Tour stand at Madison Square Garden, Bruce brings his sixteen-year-old kid sister Pam on stage during the last encore.

1984: Bruce joins La Bamba and the Hubcaps on stage at The Stone Pony in Asbury Park for “Travelin’ Band” and “I’m Bad, I’m Nationwide.”

1985: At Night Four at Giants Stadium on the Born in the U.S.A. Tour, Little Steven makes a guest appearance during the encores. This show is available as part of Bruce’s official archive series–get it here.

1987: Bruce makes a surprise appearance at The Stone Pony for a second night in a row–this time joining Levon Helm’s All-Stars for “Up on Cripple Creek” and “Lucille.”

1992: Bruce and his new touring band bring World Tour 1992 to the Richfield Coliseum for the second of two nights.

1999: Bruce and the E Street Band play their second of five Reunion shows at Boston’s Fleet Center.

2002: Bruce appears on the cover of the new issue of Rolling Stone.

2009: Bruce brings the Working on a Dream Tour to Mansfield, Massachusetts, for the first of two shows. Jay Weinberg takes the drums for the encores.

2018: Springsteen on Broadway continues its theatrical run at the Walter Kerr Theater in New York City.

excerpt.rendered: On this date: Bruce makes a guest appearance with Levon Helm, Little Steven reunites with the E Street Band, and the original Miami Horns make their final appearance on an E Street stage.

jetpack-related-posts.title: August 21
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Guest appearances galore on this date in history: Flo and Eddie, Little Steven and the Disciples of Soul, Marah's Dave Bielanko, and more. Plus: the premiere of the modern arrangement of "Dancing in the Dark" and more.

jetpack-related-posts.title: August 19
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: This date in history was the source of five tracks from Live 1975-85 plus the official video for Born to Run. Plus: Bruce brings back two Tunnel of Love tour staples after a 20-year absence, and more.

jetpack-related-posts.title: August 1
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Bruce kicks off August with a bang: from the debut of the Miami Horns, the first-ever performances of "Something in the Night," "Two for the Road," "Rendezvous," and "Trouble River," a Letterman appearance, bar gigs and more--this date in history is jam-packed! (And yet Bruce still finds time to take…

jetpack-related-posts.title: Kingdom of Days: December 16
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: It's the debut of "Brothers Under the Bridge," the release of "The Wrestler," and the last "Crush on You" for 28 years as Bruce's tours start to wind down for the year.

jetpack-related-posts.title: September 14
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Both River tours ended on this date; check out highlights from each inside, plus: Ed Norton interviews Bruce on stage in Toronto, and on the Rising Tour, Bruce starts rediscovering his missing years.

jetpack-related-posts.title: October 26
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Listen in on Bruce's first-ever show in Seattle, Darkness Tour rehearsals, and more--all from this date.
